voice_pattern: Talks in questions more than statements — even commands come out as questions

speech_card.under_pressure: questions become rhetorical and rapid-fire, barely waiting for answers
speech_card.never_articulates: direct demands — always frames wants as questions to maintain deniability
speech_card.deflection_style: answers a question with another question, redirecting without ever committing

voice_pattern: Short sentences, drops pronouns and articles — telegraphic speech

speech_card.under_pressure: compresses to single words or two-word fragments
speech_card.never_articulates: full explanations of reasoning — just states conclusions
speech_card.deflection_style: goes silent, then changes subject with a flat non-sequitur

voice_pattern: Overly formal, never uses contractions — speaks like a written document

speech_card.under_pressure: formality becomes rigid and clipped, each word precisely chosen
speech_card.never_articulates: casual affection — warmth comes out as stiff courtesy
speech_card.deflection_style: retreats into procedure, protocol, or technicality

voice_pattern: Rambles then catches himself — starts long, abruptly stops or redirects

speech_card.never_articulates: the thing he was actually getting at — always veers off before landing
speech_card.deflection_style: buries the real point inside a longer tangent so it sounds incidental

voice_pattern: Deadpan delivery — says serious things flatly, jokes without changing tone

speech_card.under_pressure: gets quieter instead of louder, tone goes completely flat
speech_card.never_articulates: that he cares — everything is presented as observation, not feeling
speech_card.deflection_style: dry understatement that makes the other person unsure if it was a joke

voice_pattern: Starts sentences then changes direction mid-thought — visible thinking out loud

speech_card.under_pressure: sentences fragment more, thoughts collide and overlap
speech_card.never_articulates: a clean thesis — always arrives at the point sideways
speech_card.deflection_style: genuinely loses the thread, or pretends to, circling back later when it's less charged

voice_pattern: Speaks in stories and analogies — answers questions with anecdotes

speech_card.under_pressure: stories get shorter and sharper, parables become pointed warnings
speech_card.never_articulates: direct instructions — always expects the listener to extract the lesson
speech_card.deflection_style: launches into a tangentially related story that avoids the actual question

voice_pattern: Finishes other people's sentences — impatient listener who jumps ahead

speech_card.under_pressure: interrupts constantly, stops listening, talks over others
speech_card.never_articulates: patience or uncertainty — always projects that he already knows
speech_card.deflection_style: preemptively answers what he thinks you're about to ask, often wrong

voice_pattern: Qualifies everything — hedges, conditionals, 'sort of', 'I guess', 'probably'

speech_card.under_pressure: hedging disappears, suddenly direct and blunt — the real voice
speech_card.never_articulates: certainty about anything emotional — keeps an escape hatch in every statement
speech_card.deflection_style: adds so many qualifiers that the original point dissolves into mush

voice_pattern: Echoes the last thing someone said before responding — processing out loud

speech_card.under_pressure: echoing becomes sarcastic repetition, throwing words back as weapons
speech_card.never_articulates: original emotional statements — always reacts to what others said first
speech_card.deflection_style: repeats the question back slowly, buying time while appearing engaged

voice_pattern: Gives everyone nicknames and uses them consistently — never uses real names

speech_card.under_pressure: drops nicknames, uses real name — which signals how serious the situation is
speech_card.never_articulates: genuine respect directly — it's buried in the nicknames he chooses
speech_card.deflection_style: makes a joke about the person's nickname to change the subject

voice_pattern: Speaks in instructions and lists — 'first, second, third' even in casual talk

speech_card.under_pressure: lists break down, order collapses, reveals the chaos underneath the structure
speech_card.never_articulates: feelings without numbering them — 'I have two problems with this'
speech_card.deflection_style: reframes emotional questions as procedural ones with action items

voice_pattern: Trails off at the end of sentences — leaves thoughts hanging for others to complete

speech_card.under_pressure: sentences get even more incomplete, almost non-verbal
speech_card.deflection_style: starts the hard thing, trails off, switches to something safer

voice_pattern: Says exactly what he means with no softening — no 'maybe', no 'I think'

speech_card.under_pressure: doesn't change at all — already at maximum directness
speech_card.never_articulates: doubt or ambiguity — presents everything as settled fact
speech_card.deflection_style: states something so directly that it shuts down the conversation

voice_pattern: Mutters asides and commentary meant to be overheard — passive-aggressive honesty

speech_card.under_pressure: asides become louder and more pointed, barely disguised as mumbling
speech_card.never_articulates: complaints directly to the person — always through the aside channel
speech_card.deflection_style: pretends he wasn't talking to anyone when called on it
